title: Method and system for seismic surveying
abstract: A method for seismic surveying comprises performing a towed seismic survey by towing seismic cables and seismic sources from a vessel and performing a Vertical Seismic Profiling (VSP) simultaneously to the towed seismic survey and using the same seismic sources.
claims: Claims 1. Method for seismic surveying comprising - performing a towed seismic survey by towing seismic cables and seismic sources from a vessel, - performing a Vertical Seismic Profiling (VSP) simultaneously to the towed seismic survey and using the same seismic sources. 2. Method according to claim 1, where the seismic cables are DAS cables. 3. Method according to claim 1 or 2, where DAS cables comprises at least two fiber optic cables arranged in parallel and connected at their ends. 4. Method according to one of the previous claims, where the DAS cable comprises a main cable and at least one sub cable, where the sub cables are arranged substantially perpendicular to the main cable. 5. Method according to claim 4, where the angle between the sub cable and the main cable ensures that the angle between the incident seismic signal and the sub cable is less than 90 degrees. 6. Method according to one of claims 2-5, where signals are recorded in the cable with a distance of 25 cm. 7. Method according to one of the previous claims, where the towed seismic survey is a near bottom seismic survey. 8. Method according to one of claims 1-6, where the towed seismic survey is a seabed seismic survey. 9. System for performing seismic surveying comprising a towed seismic survey where seismic cables and seismic sources are towed by a vessel, a Vertical Seismic Profiling (VSP) comprising a cable arranged in a borehole, means for simultaneously performing the VSP and the towed seismic survey by using the same seismic sources. 10. System according to claim 9, where the seismic cables are DAS cables. 1 1. System according to claim 9 or 10, where DAS cables comprises at least two fiber optic cables arranged in parallel and connected at their ends. 12. System according to one of claims 9-12, where the DAS cable comprises a main cable and at least one sub cable, where the sub cables are arranged substantially perpendicular to the main cable. 13. System according to claim 12, where the angle between the sub cable and the main cable ensures that the angle between the incident seismic signal and the sub cable is less than 90 degrees. 14. System according to one of claims 9-13, where signals are recorded in the cable with a distance of 25 cm. 15. System according to one of claims 9-14, where the towed seismic survey is a near bottom seismic survey. 16. System according to one of claims 9-14, where the towed seismic survey is a seabed seismic survey. 17. System according to one of claims 12-16 where the sub cable comprises weights and/or control fins.
